name: Credit.Org - Arlington, Tx Branch
description_short: Non-profit credit and housing counselor offering free debt relief, credit building, and HUD-approved homeownership guidance since 1974.
description_long:
Credit.org is a nationally-operating nonprofit organization founded in 1974 that provides free financial counseling and housing assistance services. The organization has delivered $2.2 billion in homeowner support and completed over 5.2 million counseling sessions, positioning itself as an established player in non-profit credit counseling. The Arlington, TX branch operates as part of this larger network offering localized access to certified counselors.

The organization offers comprehensive services across five main categories: debt relief and management, credit building and repair, reverse mortgage counseling (including HUD-required sessions), housing assistance (pre-purchase, foreclosure prevention, rental readiness), and disaster recovery financial planning. They provide both one-on-one counseling sessions and self-directed education through their FIT Academy platform, live classes, and financial calculators. Services are delivered through scheduled appointments with certified counselors who develop personalized financial plans.

Credit.org distinguishes itself through its non-profit structure with no financial incentive to upsell services, multi-language support (English and Spanish), HUD-approval for housing counseling, and a 50-year operational history. The organization emphasizes accessibility through free initial consultations and a three-step process (schedule, plan, execute) designed to reduce complexity. They also offer specialized services rarely found in the industry, including reverse mortgage counseling and post-disaster financial recovery planning.

While the website demonstrates credibility and scale, potential limitations include limited transparency about specific outcomes or success rates for individual service categories, no visible pricing breakdowns (though services appear free), and geographic service availability constraints that may not extend to all areas despite the national presence. The organization's reliance on a counselor-dependent model means quality may vary by individual advisor.
pros: Non-profit structure eliminates sales pressure and financial conflicts of interest
HUD-approved housing counselors and NFCC-certified debt counselors available
Free initial consultations and personalized financial plans at no cost
50-year operational history (since 1974) with $2.2B in homeowner support delivered
Specialized services including reverse mortgage counseling and disaster recovery planning
Bilingual support (English and Spanish) for broader accessibility
Multiple learning formats: live classes, FIT Academy courses, webinars, calculators, and one-on-one counseling
cons: Limited transparency on website about specific success rates or outcomes by service type
Counselor quality may vary across locations; experience heavily dependent on assigned advisor match
Geographic service availability unclear—national organization but specific coverage areas not detailed on main site
No clear information about wait times for appointments or service turnaround
Website lacks detailed case studies or specific client outcome metrics beyond aggregate numbers
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking free pre-purchase counseling and down payment guidance
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need HUD-approved counseling and prevention strategies
Individuals with high debt seeking free debt management plans without sales-driven upselling
Seniors considering reverse mortgages who need HUD-required counseling from certified advisors
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for consumers seeking unbiased, free financial counseling from non-profit certified advisors without pressure to purchase additional products or services. The primary caveat is that quality and availability depend on geographic location and individual counselor assignment, and the organization's success is not transparently documented through published outcome metrics on their website.
services: Free debt relief and debt management counseling with personalized plans
Credit building and credit score improvement sessions
Credit report review and credit dispute guidance
HUD-approved reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and non-HECM)
Pre-purchase homeownership counseling and down payment education
Foreclosure prevention and mortgage default assistance
Rental readiness counseling and rental assistance when rent is unaffordable
Post-disaster financial recovery and rebuilding assistance
FIT Academy self-paced courses on budgeting, debt, credit, mortgages, and homeownership
Live interactive classes on credit, debt, and housing topics
Financial calculators for budgeting, debt management, and retirement planning
Workplace financial wellness events and employee financial education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2000 E Lamar Blvd, Suite 600, Arlington, TX 76006
